steps.title: Use the side ratio
steps.content:
The side lengths $KL$ and $ST$ are both $28$, and
$$\frac{KL}{KJ}=\frac{ST}{SR}$$Substitute $28$ for both numerators.

steps.title: Apply side angle side
steps.content: The triangles already have $KL=ST$, $KJ=SR$, and the included angles $K$ and $S$ are both $40^\circ$. That proves the triangles congruent by side-angle-side without any additional information. The answer is D.
